summary: Item-system decomp PR split from #2345 that replaced placeholder stubs with ten item-related 100% matched functions, updated related item structs, includes, and prototypes, and received an approval with no inline review comments in the loaded slice.

key_files.role: Implemented 100% matched itHitodeman_UnkMotion1_Anim and recovered an attribute field at x48.
Implemented 100% matched it_802CA074 transition helper using JObj flags, motion change, hitlag callbacks, scale, and sfx.
Implemented 100% matched it_802CAE94 scale-up tick with external f64 constants.
Implemented 100% matched itKirbycutterbeam_UnkMotion0_Phys using trigonometric velocity derivation, speed decay, visibility flags, and PAD_STACK.
Implemented 100% matched itMewtwoshadowball_UnkMotion17_Anim and used recovered item var x64 for active hitbox scaling.
Implemented 100% matched itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Phys using ItemLink matrix setup, hit test call, velocity zeroing, and Ness yoyo_hitbox_pos.
Implemented 100% matched it_802E27B4 launch helper and added includes for freeze item, camera, and random dependencies.
Implemented 100% matched it_802E16F8 spawn helper with item kind 0xD4, facing setup, collision facing, motion changes, and jumped_on callback.
Changed red shell code including matched itRshell_UnkMotion5_Anim per PR body; diff excerpt shows local temp introduction in it_8028CFE0 but full hunk is truncated.
Changed green shell code for 100% matched itZrshell_UnkMotion6_Phys per PR body; loaded diff excerpt does not include full hunk.
Recovered item-var and attribute fields for Kyasarin, Mewtwo Shadowball, Oldottosea, and Great Fox Laser structs.
Recovered typed f32 fields in itClimbersIceAttributes, replacing part of a padding block.
Updated yoyo helper prototypes from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to typed ItemLink, Vec3, itYoyoAttributes, Item, and return types; added necessary item headers.
Updated call to it_802C1854 to pass Item_GObj.
Updated it_802C1854 prototype to bool it_802C1854(Item_GObj* gobj).
Updated it_8028EB88 prototype by removing an Item* parameter and typing the remaining parameters.
Updated it_802D4C74 prototype from unknown signature to void it_802D4C74(Item_GObj* gobj).
Updated it_802D208C prototype from unknown signature to void it_802D208C(Item_GObj*).
Updated it_802B3554 prototype from unknown signature to void it_802B3554(Item_GObj* gobj, Fighter_GObj* owner).
Added includes for itcoll, lb_00B0, and Runtime/runtime, likely to support newly typed Great Fox Laser item vars or nearby code.
Added includes for itkyasarinegg and baselib/random, alongside Kyasarin struct field recovery.

what_changed: PR body states it was split from #2345 and scoped to item-related 100% matches only.

Ten functions were listed by the author as 100%: itHitodeman_UnkMotion1_Anim, it_802CA074, it_802CAE94, itKirbycutterbeam_UnkMotion0_Phys, itMewtwoshadowball_UnkMotion17_Anim, itNessyoyo_UnkMotion3_Phys, it_802E27B4, it_802E16F8, itRshell_UnkMotion5_Anim, and itZrshell_UnkMotion6_Phys.
The bot report recorded 13 new matches, including the ten code functions plus .sdata/.sdata2 matches for itnessyoyo and itkabigon, and reported matched code +2976 bytes.
Several headers were updated to replace UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or incorrect prototypes with typed signatures used by the new matched bodies.
Item vars and attributes in itCharItems.h and itCommonItems.h were refined from padding to typed fields where matched bodies accessed offsets.
Some functions retained narrow matching tactics such as PAD_STACK, UNUSED stack bytes, f32 pad arrays, local temp variables, and extern constants.

smart_moves.text: Kept PR scope narrow by separating item-related 100% matches from #2345 rather than mixing them with broader pending work.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text: Split from #2345 — item-related 100% matches only

smart_moves.text: Updated owning headers alongside recovered bodies so callsites and prototypes reflected the proven signatures.
Recovered typed item fields instead of leaving broad padding where matched functions needed specific offsets.
Used existing helpers and subsystem operations in matched bodies, including GET_ITEM, Item_80268E5C, HSD_JObjSetScale, mpCollSetFacingDir, PSMTXConcat, and item hitlag callbacks.

decomp_lessons.lesson: When a matched item body proves an offset's type, update the item-var or attribute struct field and the relevant function prototype rather than preserving raw unknown signatures.
Item motion helpers often require paired body and header cleanup: implementing the function can expose exact Item_GObj, Vec3, ItemLink, Fighter_GObj, and return types in nearby declarations.
Small stack-shape tactics such as PAD_STACK or UNUSED local arrays may appear in successful matches, but this PR evidence only supports them as narrow per-function tactics, not as general style.

naming_conventions.name: item function names
naming_conventions.observation: The PR retained a mix of semantic motion names such as itHitodeman_UnkMotion1_Anim and address-style names such as it_802CA074 or it_802E27B4 where stronger semantics were not promoted into symbol names.

naming_conventions.name: recovered struct fields
naming_conventions.observation: Recovered item fields use offset-style names such as x48, x64, x2C, x14, and x24 rather than guessed semantic names.

naming_conventions.name: local variables
naming_conventions.observation: Locals follow short role names such as ip, jobj, attrs, attr, pos, vel, scale, link1, link2, owner, fp, and coll_facing when roles are clear.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: PAD_STACK used in several newly matched item functions to satisfy stack layout.
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Loaded evidence shows successful 100% matches, but does not include local objdiff details for why each PAD_STACK was necessary.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: External float/double constants were declared for constants whose storage affected matching.
evidence_refs: diff: itkamex.c extern const f64 it_804DD358/it_804DD360
diff: itnessyoyo.c extern const f32 it_804DD150
diff: itoldottosea.c extern const f32 it_804DD73C/it_804DD728
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: Treat as function-specific matching evidence, not a general instruction to externalize literals.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Uniform Vec3 scale assignment order was written explicitly before HSD_JObjSetScale in matched JObj scale code.
evidence_refs: diff: itkamex.c HSD_JObjGetScale then per-axis increments

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Matrix identity plus JObj matrix concat was used to recover yoyo link world position before hit detection.
A temporary local was introduced for expression shape in itrshell.c.
evidence_refs: diff: itrshell.c excerpt temp = -ip->facing_dir; v.x *= temp
assembly_or_matching_tactics.caution: The full changed hunk is truncated, so no broader conclusion should be drawn.

notes: PR identity, merge status, changed files, author-stated 100% matches, bot match report, and approval are well supported by loaded metadata and text corpus.
The diff excerpt is truncated; it contains full visible hunks for many files but not the complete itrshell.c and itzgshell.c changes.
Function behavior descriptions under 'What Claude thinks these functions do' are PR-body text and should be treated as author-provided explanatory notes, not independently verified decomp facts.
No inline review comments are present in the loaded slice; review intent beyond approval is unsupported.
No local build logs, objdiff command output, or reviewer discussion are loaded beyond the bot report.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Matched item bodies can justify typed item vars and truthful prototypes
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2406, several item 100% matches landed together with struct field recovery and header prototype updates. Candidate lesson: when a matched item function proves offsets and call signatures, update the relevant item-var/attribute structs and owning headers rather than leaving padding or UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Treat PAD_STACK and dummy stack locals as narrow matching tactics
accepted_candidate_records.text: PR #2406 includes successful 100% matches that retain PAD_STACK or UNUSED stack bytes in specific functions. Candidate tactic record: these can be evidence-backed per-function stack-shape tactics, but should not be promoted as preferred style without objdiff context.

source_update_candidates.title: Header cleanup should accompany proven item body signatures
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2406 is a good example for the existing truthful-headers-and-includes standard: matched item bodies were paired with updates from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or incorrect signatures to typed prototypes in the owning headers.
source_update_candidates.reason: Potential illustrative example for an existing standard; not a new rule.

source_update_candidates.title: Do not overgeneralize extern constants from code matches
source_update_candidates.text: PR #2406 used extern constants in several matched functions. Curator may consider adding this as an example under literals/data ownership or matching tactics: extern constants can be part of a match when backed by section evidence, but the PR slice alone does not prove a broad standard.
source_update_candidates.reason: Possible example only; loaded evidence lacks full section ownership detail.

rejection_notes: Do not promote the PR-body 'What Claude thinks these functions do' descriptions as accepted semantic names or canonical behavior without independent source evidence.
Do not infer that PAD_STACK, UNUSED arrays, or pad locals are generally preferred style; the PR only shows they were used in specific 100% matched functions.
Do not claim all 21 changed files contain complete loaded diffs; raw_diff is marked truncated and itrshell/itzgshell details are incomplete in the excerpt.
Do not infer reviewer rationale from the empty approval review.
